note: Thin library submodule registering the Billboard.js Chart backend for the charts module. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded); no site mutation.

evals.prompt: I want my Charts module charts to render with Billboard.js. Which submodule do I enable and what library id does it register?
evals.expected_output: Enable charts_billboard; it registers the Chart library plugin id `billboard` (Billboard.js), then select Billboard.js as the library.

evals.prompt: Which external JavaScript libraries does charts_billboard require?
evals.expected_output: Billboard.js 3.10.3 and D3 7.9.0 (Billboard.js is a D3-based fork of C3), loaded from CDN by default.

evals.prompt: How do I serve the Billboard.js and D3 files locally instead of from a CDN with charts_billboard?
evals.expected_output: Disable the CDN option in Charts advanced settings and install billboard/d3 into /libraries via Composer (billboardjs/billboard, d3/d3) or the npm libraries:copy script.

evals.prompt: In a Charts render array, how do I force a chart to use Billboard.js?
evals.expected_output: Set '#chart_library' => 'billboard' on the chart element (or select Billboard.js in the Views style / block / field, or as the site default library).

evals.prompt: Does Billboard.js support candlestick and gauge charts through the Charts module?
evals.expected_output: Yes — billboard supports area, arearange, bar, bubble, candlestick, column, donut, gauge, line, pie, scatter, and spline.
